text:
Explore (Digging In)

Providing for Individual Needs

Suggested Questions

  • What patterns do you see in the rate table?

Some students will only see that the number of cups of popcorn adds by 4. If students are not seeing the multiplicative pattern, ask more questions.

  • If we know that 1 ounce of popcorn kernels makes 4 cups of popcorn, how much popcorn can you make with 2 ounces of popcorn kernels?
  • How do you know?
  • What is the pattern on the table?
  • How could I find how many cups of popcorn I could make with 15 ounces of kernels?

• 8 cups
  • For every 1 ounce of kernels, you get 4 cups of popcorn. So if you double the ounces of kernels, it will double how much popcorn you get.
  • As the number of ounces of kernels increases by 1, the number of cups of popcorn increases by 4.
  • Some students will continue to add 4s. Other students will see the multiplicative pattern and multiply by 4.

For students who are seeing an additive pattern rather than multiplicative pattern, continue to ask about other amounts of kernels.

  • How could I find how many cups of popcorn I could make with 20 ounces of kernels? 27 ounces of kernels? 36 ounces of kernels? For any number of ounces of kernels?
Multiply the number of ounces of kernels by 4.

Planning for the Summary

What evidence will you use in the summary to clarify and deepen understanding of the Now What Do You Know? question? What will you do if you do not have evidence?

Now What Do You Know?

What are the advantages of using rate tables and unit rates to solve problems? How might an algebraic expression or equation be useful?

As you are circulating during the Explore, listen for how students are describing using the rate tables to find unit rates and equations. Find examples of student work and conversations to use in the summary to help students build expertise in working with unit rates to solve ratio problems.
